text: We present a case of laparoscopic repair of paracetamide-induced vesicle vagina fistula in metastatic colon cancer. A fifty-four years old female had a second adenocarcinoma with peritoneal seeding over the cul-de-sac and uterus. We performed right hemicolectomy, seeding tumor excision, hysterectomy, and bilateral saphingo-o-phorectomy.
The final pathology was stage IVB, so we started Bevacizumab Plus for theory biweekly. However, the patient complained of urine from the vagina after four courses of the treatment.

The scissor graphy reveals a vesicle vagina fistula approximately three millimeters in length. The corposcope shows a fistula opening on the left aspect of the vaginal stump, measuring about eight millimeters, with urine visibly leaking from the opening.

After full course of the treatment, abdominal CT scan shows stable disease without tumor recurrence. So we decided to do laparoscopic repair of the fistula after discontinuation of Bevacizumab for three months.

A cystoscope is used to visualize the bladder, revealing the fistula opening located at the trigone near the left ureteral orifice. A guide wire is inserted from the vaginal stump and advanced into the bladder.
The surgeon begins dissecting the bladder base from the vagina stump, revealing the location of the guide wire amidst adhesions between the bladder and vagina stump.

The surgeon creates a cystotomy on the left aspect of the bladder, revealing the fistula opening inside the bladder cavity. Dissection proceeds towards the posterior wall of the bladder to expose the fistula tract externally.

The surgeon extends the cystotomy edge to expose the vaginal opening of the fistula. The fibrotic tissue is removed, and the edge of the vaginal opening is trimmed. The opening is then closed using a Vigrio absorbable suture.

The surgeon closes the cystotomy site with 3-0 Monocryl absorbable sutures. Post-surgery, the patient shows no signs of urine leakage from the vagina, and a follow-up cystography one month later confirms no recurrence of the vesicovaginal fistula.
